threat.title: Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) Remote Code Execution
threat.description: Server-Side Template Injection occurs when user-controlled input is embedded into template engines (Jinja2, Freemarker, Velocity, ERB) without proper sanitization, allowing attackers to inject template directives that execute arbitrary code on the server. This vulnerability enables complete system compromise through remote code execution, data exfiltration, and persistent backdoor installation.

prerequisites: User input is rendered through template engine without sanitization
Template engine allows access to dangerous objects/methods (no sandboxing)
Application uses server-side rendering with dynamic template compilation
Template autoescaping is disabled or bypassed
Error messages reveal template engine type and version

cve_references.description: Uber Node.js application SSTI vulnerability in template rendering leading to RCE

mitigations.title: Implement Jinja2 Sandboxed Environment with Strict Autoescaping
mitigations.description: Use Jinja2's SandboxedEnvironment to restrict access to dangerous objects and methods, combined with strict autoescaping to prevent HTML and code injection. This prevents attackers from accessing Python internals like __class__, __mro__, and subprocess.

code_example.code:
from flask import Flask, render_template_string, request, escape from jinja2.sandbox import SandboxedEnvironment from jinja2 import select_autoescape from markupsafe import Markup import re app = Flask(__name__) # Create sandboxed environment with strict autoescaping sandbox = SandboxedEnvironment( autoescape=select_autoescape( enabled_extensions=('html', 'xml', 'j2', 'jinja2'), default_for_string=True, # CRITICAL: Escape all strings by default ), # Disable dangerous features trim_blocks=True, lstrip_blocks=True, ) # Whitelist of allowed template variables (principle of least privilege) ALLOWED_TEMPLATE_VARS = { 'user_name', 'order_id', 'product_name', 'date', 'amount' } def sanitize_template_input(user_input): """ Validate and sanitize user input before template rendering. Block template syntax characters entirely. """ # Block template delimiters dangerous_patterns = [ r'{{', r'}}', # Jinja2 expression r'{%', r'%}', # Jinja2 statement r'{#', r'#}', # Jinja2 comment r'__', # Python dunder methods r'\.', # Attribute access (be careful with this) r'\[', r'\]', # Subscript access ] for pattern in dangerous_patterns: if re.search(pattern, user_input): raise ValueError(f"Invalid characters detected: {pattern}") return user_input @app.route('/email/preview', methods=['POST']) def preview_email(): """ SECURE: Email template preview with sandboxing """ try: # Get user input template_vars = request.json # Validate only whitelisted variables are present invalid_vars = set(template_vars.keys()) - ALLOWED_TEMPLATE_VARS if invalid_vars: return {"error": f"Invalid template variables: {invalid_vars}"}, 400 # Sanitize all inputs sanitized_vars = { key: sanitize_template_input(str(value)) for key, value in template_vars.items() } # Use pre-compiled template (NEVER compile user input as template) template_source = """

Order Confirmation

Dear {{ user_name }},

Your order #{{ order_id }} for {{ product_name }} has been confirmed.

Amount: ${{ amount }}

Date: {{ date }}

""" # Render using sandboxed environment template = sandbox.from_string(template_source) rendered = template.render(**sanitized_vars) return {"preview": rendered}, 200 except ValueError as e: return {"error": str(e)}, 400 except Exception as e: app.logger.error(f"Template rendering error: {e}") return {"error": "Template rendering failed"}, 500 # Alternative: Use pre-compiled templates from files (RECOMMENDED) @app.route('/email/send', methods=['POST']) def send_email(): """ BEST PRACTICE: Load templates from files, never from user input """ template_vars = request.json # Validate inputs sanitized_vars = { key: escape(value) for key, value in template_vars.items() if key in ALLOWED_TEMPLATE_VARS } # Render from pre-defined template file rendered = render_template_string( 'email_order_confirmation.html', **sanitized_vars ) return {"status": "sent"}, 200 if __name__ == '__main__': app.run(debug=False) # Never run debug mode in production

nist_csf: PR.IP-2 - A System Development Life Cycle to manage systems is implemented
PR.DS-5 - Protections against data leaks are implemented
validation_steps: Test with SSTI payload: {{7*7}} - should render as literal '{{7*7}}', not '49'
Test with Python introspection: {{''.__class__}} - should raise SecurityError
Verify autoescaping: {{<script>alert(1)</script>}} - should render as escaped HTML
Check sandbox restrictions: {{config}} - should be undefined
Validate whitelist enforcement: Submit non-whitelisted variable - should be rejected

mitigations.title: Use Precompiled Handlebars Templates with Safe Compilation
mitigations.description: For Node.js applications, precompile Handlebars templates at build time and disable runtime compilation. This prevents user input from being interpreted as template code.
nist_csf: PR.IP-2 - A System Development Life Cycle to manage systems is implemented
validation_steps: Verify all templates are precompiled at build/startup time
Test that runtime template compilation is disabled
Attempt SSTI payload in data fields - should be escaped in output
Verify only whitelisted template names can be accessed
Check that no user input reaches handlebars.compile()

mitigations.title: Implement Freemarker Configuration Restrictions
mitigations.description: For Java applications using Freemarker, configure strict template permissions, disable dangerous built-ins, and use template whitelisting to prevent code execution.
nist_csf: PR.IP-2 - A System Development Life Cycle to manage systems is implemented
validation_steps: Verify ?api built-in is disabled: Test ${"freemarker.template.utility.Execute"?new()} - should fail
Test autoescaping: ${"<script>alert(1)</script>"} - should be HTML-escaped
Confirm templates load from classpath only, not user-supplied paths
Validate input sanitization rejects template syntax in data fields
Test with payload: ${Class.forName("java.lang.Runtime")} - should be blocked

mitigations.title: Deploy SSTI Detection with ModSecurity WAF Rules
mitigations.description: Implement Web Application Firewall (WAF) rules to detect and block common SSTI payloads before they reach the application. This provides defense-in-depth alongside secure coding practices.

code_example.code:
# ModSecurity SSTI Detection Rules
# Add to /etc/modsecurity/rules/ssti-rules.conf

# Detect Jinja2 template injection patterns
SecRule REQUEST_COOKIES|REQUEST_COOKIES_NAMES|REQUEST_FILENAME|ARGS_NAMES|ARGS|XML:/* \
  "@rx \\{\\{.*?(?:__class__|__mro__|__subclasses__|__globals__|__builtins__|__import__|config|request|lipsum|cycler|joiner|namespace).*?\\}\\}" \
  "id:941380,\
  phase:2,\
  block,\
  capture,\
  t:none,t:urlDecodeUni,\
  msg:'Jinja2 SSTI Attack Detected',\
  logdata:'Matched Data: %{TX.0} found within %{MATCHED_VAR_NAME}: %{MATCHED_VAR}',\
  tag:'application-multi',\
  tag:'language-python',\
  tag:'platform-multi',\
  tag:'attack-injection',\
  tag:'OWASP_CRS',\
  tag:'OWASP_CRS/WEB_ATTACK/INJECTION',\
  tag:'WASCTC/WASC-19',\
  tag:'OWASP_TOP_10/A1',\
  tag:'PCI/6.5.2',\
  severity:'CRITICAL',\
  setvar:'tx.anomaly_score_pl1=+%{tx.critical_anomaly_score}',\
  setvar:'tx.sql_injection_score=+%{tx.critical_anomaly_score}',\
  setvar:'ip.ssti_block_flag=1',\
  expirevar:'ip.ssti_block_flag=3600'"

# Detect Freemarker template injection
SecRule REQUEST_COOKIES|REQUEST_COOKIES_NAMES|REQUEST_FILENAME|ARGS_NAMES|ARGS|XML:/* \
  "@rx (?:<#|\\$\\{|#\\{).*?(?:freemarker\\.template|Class\\.forName|Runtime|ProcessBuilder|Execute|assign|import).*?(?:>|\\})" \
  "id:941381,\
  phase:2,\
  block,\
  capture,\
  t:none,t:urlDecodeUni,t:htmlEntityDecode,\
  msg:'Freemarker SSTI Attack Detected',\
  logdata:'Matched Data: %{TX.0} found within %{MATCHED_VAR_NAME}: %{MATCHED_VAR}',\
  tag:'application-multi',\
  tag:'language-java',\
  tag:'attack-injection',\
  severity:'CRITICAL',\
  setvar:'tx.anomaly_score_pl1=+%{tx.critical_anomaly_score}',\
  setvar:'ip.ssti_block_flag=1',\
  expirevar:'ip.ssti_block_flag=3600'"

# Detect ERB template injection (Ruby)
SecRule REQUEST_COOKIES|REQUEST_COOKIES_NAMES|REQUEST_FILENAME|ARGS_NAMES|ARGS|XML:/* \
  "@rx <%=?.*?(?:system|exec|eval|`|%x|IO\\.popen|Kernel|File|Dir|require|load).*?%>" \
  "id:941382,\
  phase:2,\
  block,\
  capture,\
  t:none,t:urlDecodeUni,\
  msg:'ERB SSTI Attack Detected',\
  logdata:'Matched Data: %{TX.0} found within %{MATCHED_VAR_NAME}: %{MATCHED_VAR}',\
  tag:'application-multi',\
  tag:'language-ruby',\
  tag:'attack-injection',\
  severity:'CRITICAL',\
  setvar:'tx.anomaly_score_pl1=+%{tx.critical_anomaly_score}'"

# Detect Velocity template injection
SecRule REQUEST_COOKIES|REQUEST_COOKIES_NAMES|REQUEST_FILENAME|ARGS_NAMES|ARGS|XML:/* \
  "@rx \\#set\\(.*?(?:Runtime|Class|ProcessBuilder).*?\\)" \
  "id:941383,\
  phase:2,\
  block,\
  capture,\
  t:none,t:urlDecodeUni,\
  msg:'Velocity SSTI Attack Detected',\
  severity:'CRITICAL',\
  setvar:'tx.anomaly_score_pl1=+%{tx.critical_anomaly_score}'"

# Block rate limiting for detected SSTI attempts
SecRule IP:SSTI_BLOCK_FLAG "@eq 1" \
  "id:941385,\
  phase:1,\
  block,\
  msg:'Client blocked due to SSTI attack attempts',\
  severity:'WARNING'"

# Azure Application Gateway WAF Custom Rule (JSON format)
# Add via Azure Portal > Application Gateway > Web Application Firewall > Custom Rules
# {
#   "name": "BlockSSTI",
#   "priority": 10,
#   "ruleType": "MatchRule",
#   "matchConditions": [
#     {
#       "matchVariables": [
#         {"variableName": "RequestUri"},
#         {"variableName": "QueryString"},
#         {"variableName": "PostArgs"}
#       ],
#       "operator": "Regex",
#       "matchValues": [
#         ".*\\{\\{.*(__class__|__mro__|__import__).*\\}\\}.*",
#         ".*\\$\\{.*freemarker\\.template.*\\}.*",
#         ".*<%=.*system\\(.*%>.*"
#       ],
#       "transforms": ["UrlDecode", "HtmlEntityDecode"]
#     }
#   ],
#   "action": "Block"
# }

# AWS WAF Rule (Terraform)
# resource "aws_wafv2_web_acl" "ssti_protection" {
#   name  = "ssti-protection"
#   scope = "REGIONAL"
#   
#   default_action {
#     allow {}
#   }
#   
#   rule {
#     name     = "block-ssti-attempts"
#     priority = 1
#     
#     action {
#       block {}
#     }
#     
#     statement {
#       regex_pattern_set_reference_statement {
#         arn = aws_wafv2_regex_pattern_set.ssti_patterns.arn
#         
#         field_to_match {
#           all_query_arguments {}
#         }
#         
#         text_transformation {
#           priority = 0
#           type     = "URL_DECODE"
#         }
#       }
#     }
#     
#     visibility_config {
#       cloudwatch_metrics_enabled = true
#       metric_name                = "SSTIBlockedRequests"
#       sampled_requests_enabled   = true
#     }
#   }
# }

nist_csf: DE.CM-1 - The network is monitored to detect potential cybersecurity events
PR.PT-4 - Communications and control networks are protected
validation_steps: Test WAF with Jinja2 payload: {{7*7}} - should be blocked with 403
Verify ModSecurity logs capture blocked requests
Test rate limiting: Multiple SSTI attempts should trigger IP block
Confirm legitimate requests with special characters pass through
Review CloudWatch/Azure Monitor metrics for blocked SSTI attempts

indicators: HTTP requests with template syntax in parameters ({{, ${, <%, #{})
Server errors revealing template engine type/version in stack traces
Unusual outbound network connections from web server (reverse shell)
Process creation events for bash/sh/cmd.exe from web application process
Filesystem changes in web application directories (web shell installation)
Application responses containing results of template evaluation (e.g., '49' from {{7*7}})
Access to sensitive files like /etc/passwd, web.config via template injection
HTTP requests with URL-encoded or base64-encoded template payloads

log_queries.query:
// Detect SSTI attempts in HTTP requests
W3CIISLog
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where csUriQuery contains "{{" or csUriQuery contains "${" or csUriQuery contains "<%"
| extend DecodedQuery = url_decode(csUriQuery)
| where DecodedQuery matches regex @"(__class__|__mro__|__import__|freemarker\.template|Runtime|eval|exec|system)"
| summarize AttemptCount = count(), UniqueIPs = dcount(cIP) by bin(TimeGenerated, 5m), csUriStem, DecodedQuery
| where AttemptCount > 3
| project TimeGenerated, csUriStem, DecodedQuery, AttemptCount, UniqueIPs
| order by AttemptCount desc

index=web_logs sourcetype=access_combined
| rex field=uri_query "(?<template_syntax>\{\{|\$\{|<%|#\{)"
| where isnotnull(template_syntax)
| rex field=uri_query "(?<dangerous_keywords>__class__|__mro__|__import__|freemarker\\.template|Runtime|ProcessBuilder|eval|exec|system)"
| where isnotnull(dangerous_keywords)
| stats count as attempts, values(uri) as targeted_endpoints, dc(src_ip) as unique_ips by src_ip, dangerous_keywords
| where attempts > 5
| eval severity="critical"
| table _time, src_ip, attempts, dangerous_keywords, targeted_endpoints, severity

event.category: "web" AND (
  url.query: (*{{* OR *${* OR *<%* OR *#{*) AND
  url.query: (*__class__* OR *__mro__* OR *__import__* OR *freemarker.template* OR *Runtime* OR *exec* OR *eval*)
)
| stats count() by source.ip, url.path

fields @timestamp, @message
| filter @message like /\{\{|\$\{|<%/
| filter @message like /__class__|__mro__|__import__|freemarker\.template|Runtime|eval|exec/
| parse @message /^(?<client_ip>\S+) .* "(?<method>\S+) (?<uri>\S+) (?<protocol>\S+)" (?<status>\d+)/
| stats count() as attempt_count by client_ip, uri
| filter attempt_count > 3
| sort attempt_count desc

// Detect reverse shell connections from web servers
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 4688 // Process creation
| where ParentProcessName contains "w3wp.exe" or ParentProcessName contains "nginx" or ParentProcessName contains "apache"
| where NewProcessName contains "bash" or NewProcessName contains "sh" or NewProcessName contains "cmd.exe" or NewProcessName contains "powershell.exe"
| where CommandLine contains "tcp" or CommandLine contains "dev/tcp" or CommandLine contains "exec"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, User
| order by TimeGenerated desc
